popularStory.headline: Marguerite de Bavière (1363-1423)
popularStory.description:
Marguerite de Bavière, née en 1363, morte à Dijon le 23 janvier 1423, est une duchesse de Bourgogne, épouse du duc Jean Sans Peur et mère du duc Philippe III le Bon.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Margarete von Bayern Duchesse consort de Bourgogne於1363年出生在荷蘭-拉藍德丹 哈格時，她的父親Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubing27歲，母親Margaret Piast of Brieg22歲。她於1385年4月12日與Jean de Bourgogne I "sans peur", Duc de Bourgogne在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德坎布萊結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1424年1月23日在法國布格-福朗-可特艾o-達迪喬享年61歲，埋葬在法國布格-福朗-可特艾o-達迪喬。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margarete von Bayern Duchesse consort de Bourgogne于1363年出生在荷兰-拉藍德丹 哈格时，她的父亲Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubing27岁，母亲Margaret Piast of Brieg22岁。她于1385年4月12日与Jean de Bourgogne I "sans peur", Duc de Bourgogne在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德坎布萊结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1424年1月23日在法国布格-福朗-可特艾o-達迪喬享年61岁，埋葬在法国布格-福朗-可特艾o-達迪喬。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Bayern
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German (and possibly also Dutch)   Bayer   or   Beyers  , or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname. Compare   Byers  .

variants.name: Bayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Kurt, Otto, Klaus, Manfred, Bernd, Bernhard, Erwin, Franz, Hans, Johann, Juergen.

German, Scandinavian, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Bavaria (German Bayern). This region of southern Germany derives its name from that of the Celtic tribe of the Boii who once inhabited this area. They were displaced in the 6th century AD by an ancient Germanic people, the Boioarii or Baiuarii, whose name is derived from that of their Celtic predecessors. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Czechia, Slovakia, and Poland. Compare Beyer and Payer .

English (Lincolnshire): occupational name for a maker of baize cloth, from an agent derivative of Old French baies, Middle English bayes (from the adjective bai ‘reddish-brown, bay’), probably so called because of its original color. This material was said to have been introduced into Britain by immigrants from France and the Netherlands in the 16th century, but the word certainly appears much earlier in English. The surname may also be topographic denoting a ‘dweller by the bend’, from an agent derivative of Old English bēag ‘bend’.

nameMeanings.name: Margarete
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Margaret
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretha
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margarete von Bayern Duchesse consort de Bourgogne was born in 1363, in The Hague, South Holland, Netherlands, her father, Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubing, was 27 and her mother, Margaret Piast of Brieg, was 22. She married Jean de Bourgogne I "sans peur", Duc de Bourgogne on 12 April 1385, in Cambrai, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 7 daughters. She died on 23 January 1424, in Dijon, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France, at the age of 61, and was buried in Dijon, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margarete von Bayern Duchesse consort de Bourgogne est née en 1363, à La Haye, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas, son père, Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubing, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Margaret Piast of Brieg, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Jean de Bourgogne I "sans peur", Duc de Bourgogne le 12 avril 1385, à Cambrai, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée le 23 janvier 1424, à Dijon, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterrée à Dijon, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margarete von Bayern Duchesse consort de Bourgogne 1363 in Den Haag, Südholland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubing 27 und ihre Mutter Margaret Piast of Brieg 22. Sie hat am 12. April 1385 in Cambrai, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich Jean de Bourgogne I "sans peur", Duc de Bourgogne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 7 Töchter. Sie starb am 23. Januar 1424 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Dijon, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Frankreich und wurde in Dijon, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margarete von Bayern Duchesse consort de Bourgogne è nata il nel 1363 nel Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi, sua padre, Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubing, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Margaret Piast of Brieg, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Jean de Bourgogne I "sans peur", Duc de Bourgogne il 12 aprile 1385, nel Cambrai, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 7 figlie. Morì il 23 gennaio 1424, nel Dijon, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia, all'età di 61 anni, e fu sepolta a Dijon, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Margarete von Bayern Duchesse consort de Bourgogneが1363年にオランダズイド-hオランドデン ハアグで生まれた時，父Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubingは27歳，母Margaret Piast of Briegは22歳であった。1385年4月12日にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスノードカンブレイでJean de Bourgogne I "sans peur", Duc de Bourgogneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1424年1月23日にフランスボウルゴグネ-フランチェ-コムトeクoテ-ド'オルディジョンで61歳で死去し，フランスボウルゴグネ-フランチェ-コムトeクoテ-ド'オルディジョンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margarete von Bayern Duchesse consort de Bourgogne(이)가 1363년 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 든 하아그에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubing(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Piast of Brieg(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1385년 4월 12일 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 노르드 캄브라이에서 Jean de Bourgogne I "sans peur", Duc de Bourgogne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1424년 1월 23일 프랑스 부르고그니-프랭체-코트e co테-드'어 디조에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 부르고그니-프랭체-코트e co테-드'어 디조에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margarete von Bayern Duchesse consort de Bourgogne nasceu em 1363, em Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos, seu pai, Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubing, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Piast of Brieg, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Jean de Bourgogne I "sans peur", Duc de Bourgogne em 12 de abril de 1385, em Cambrai, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 7 filhas. Ela faleceu em 23 de janeiro de 1424, em Dijon, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, França, com 61 anos, e foi sepultada em Dijon, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margarete von Bayern Duchesse consort de Bourgogne родилась в 1363 г. в местности ден хааг, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды, её отцу по имени Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubing было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Piast of Brieg, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Jean de Bourgogne I "sans peur", Duc de Bourgogne 12 апреля 1385 г. в местности камбреи, норд, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 7 дочерей. Она умерла 23 января 1424 г. в местности диджон, кoте-д'ор, бургогне-фрэнче-комтe, Франция в возрасте 61 год и была похоронена в местности диджон, кoте-д'ор, бургогне-фрэнче-комтe, Франция.

sm.1404-1419: mai le 1404 e o'o I le 1419

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margarete von Bayern Duchesse consort de Bourgogne nació en 1363, en Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos, su papá, Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubing, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Margaret Piast of Brieg, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Jean de Bourgogne I "sans peur", Duc de Bourgogne el 12 de abril de 1385, en Cambrai, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 7 hijas. Murió el 23 de enero de 1424, en Dijon, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultada en Dijon, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia.
